title: Corrupt or unreadable storage is treated as empty and can overwrite all saved preferences
reasoning: loadAll swallows every read and JSON parse error and returns an empty object. A subsequent POST then saves that empty-derived object back to notifications.json, replacing all previously saved preferences with just the current request's record. This converts transient filesystem errors, partial writes, or malformed JSON into permanent data loss.
reproduction: Put invalid JSON in notifications.json or make a read fail transiently, then POST a valid preference. The handler will call loadAll(), receive {}, and save a new file containing only the posted wallet.
recommendation: Do not treat parse/read failures as an empty preference set. Return an error, preserve the existing file, log actionable details, and write via a temporary file plus atomic rename to reduce partial-file corruption.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No linked tests are included, and there is no evidence of tests covering malformed notifications.json or read failure handling.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a test with malformed notifications.json that asserts POST returns an error and does not replace the existing file contents.
minimumFixScope: Change loadAll to surface storage errors to POST/GET and prevent saveAll from running after a failed load.
